features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can inspire another creature within 60 feet. They gain a Bardic Inspiration die (d6) that they can add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw they make within the next hour. Additionally, you can use a Reaction to expend a use of Bardic Inspiration to heal a creature that has just failed a saving throw or is at 0 Hit Points.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You can cast Bard spells using Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You prepare a specific number of spells from the Bard spell list, and you can use a Musical Instrument as a spellcasting focus.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies. Your Proficiency Bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

features.name: Jack of All Trades
features.description: You can add half your Proficiency Bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your Proficiency Bonus.

features.name: Bard Subclass
features.description: You choose a Bard College that represents your artistic specialization. Your subclass grants you features at level 3 and again at levels 6 and 14.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description: You now regain all of your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Additionally, your Bardic Inspiration die increases to a d8.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your chosen Bard College.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: As a Reaction when you or an ally within 30 feet fails a saving throw against being Charmed or Frightened, you can use your performance to allow the creature to reroll the save with Advantage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: You gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You can now choose spells from the Cleric, Druid, and Wizard spell lists in addition to the Bard list whenever you learn or replace a Bard spell. Your Bardic Inspiration die also increases to a d10.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Subclass feature
features.description: You gain a feature from your chosen Bard College.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration Die (d12)
features.description: Your Bardic Inspiration die increases to a d12.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: When you roll Initiative, if you have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain two uses.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Words of Creation
features.description: You always have the Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill spells prepared. When you cast either of them, you can target a second creature within range.
